figures.caption: Figure 1: High-frequency input u(t) (left) and its derivative u′(t) (right). Here u(t) is generated as in (6) with K = 1, n = 6, aj = 1, bj = 0, and β = 0.5.

Figure 2: High-frequency input u(x, y) (left) and the corresponding nonlinear p-Laplacian −div(|∇u|p−2∇u) with p = 1.2 (right). Here u(x, y) is generated as in (7) with K = 1, n = 9, aj = 1, bj = 1, and β = 0.
